tasks.description: Install SAP dependencies, create sap-client singleton, write agent registration script, add env vars
Add SAP discovery API endpoints — let Invoica API users query the SAP agent network and surface Invoica's own SAP profile
tasks.context:
Goal: expose SAP discovery through Invoica's API. Developers using Invoica can query the broader SAP agent network to find other agents by capability, and can see Invoica's own on-chain identity.

FILE: backend/src/routes/sap.ts (NEW, max 150 lines)
  Import getSapClient from '../lib/sap-client'.

  Routes to implement:

  GET /v1/sap/network
    → Returns SAP global registry stats: totalAgents, activeAgents, totalTools
    → Call: sap.discovery.getNetworkOverview() or sap.registry.getGlobalRegistry()
    → Response: { totalAgents, activeAgents, totalTools, programId: 'SAPpUhsWLJG1FfkGRcXagEDMrMsWGjbky7AyhGpFETZ' }

  GET /v1/sap/agents?capability=payment:invoice
    → Query param: capability (required)
    → Returns: list of SAP agents with that capability (name, walletAddress, description, capabilityDetails)
    → Call: sap.discovery.findAgentsByCapability(capability) or findByCapability(capability)
    → Limit: max 20 results. Log: [sap-discovery] capability=X found=N

  GET /v1/sap/agents/me
    → Returns Invoica's own SAP profile: credentialData, capabilities, stats, agentPda
    → Uses process.env.SAP_AGENT_PDA to fetch the agent account
    → If SAP_AGENT_PDA not set → 503 { error: 'Invoica not yet registered on SAP' }
    → Call: sap.agent.get(new PublicKey(process.env.SAP_AGENT_PDA))
    → Or: sap.discovery.getAgentProfile(new PublicKey(process.env.SAP_AGENT_PDA))

  All routes:
    - Wrap in try/catch → 500 on SAP RPC error
    - Auth: require valid Invoica API key (use existing requireApiKey middleware)
    - Log all calls: [sap-routes] method=GET path=/v1/sap/...

WIRE INTO app.ts:
  Read backend/src/app.ts first to understand how routes are registered.
  Add: app.use('/v1/sap', requireApiKey, sapRouter);
  (or match existing routing pattern — could be /api/sap or similar)

FILE ADJUSTMENT NOTE:
  Check the actual method names available on the installed SapClient by reading
  node_modules/@oobe-protocol-labs/synapse-sap-sdk/dist/index.d.ts after SAP-001 installs deps.
  Map the correct discovery/agent method calls accordingly.

TEST: backend/src/routes/__tests__/sap.test.ts (NEW, max 80 lines)
  Mock getSapClient(). Test:
  - GET /v1/sap/network returns 200 with { totalAgents, programId }
  - GET /v1/sap/agents?capability=payment:invoice returns 200 with array
  - GET /v1/sap/agents without capability returns 400
  - GET /v1/sap/agents/me with SAP_AGENT_PDA unset returns 503
  - GET /v1/sap/agents/me with SAP_AGENT_PDA set returns 200

AFTER WRITING: npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep -v node_modules — 0 new errors.

review.summary: Well-structured SAP API routes with proper validation, error handling, and comprehensive tests. Code follows TypeScript best practices and project patterns.

issues.description: Hardcoded program ID should be moved to environment variable or config
Agent response mapping assumes specific property names without validation - could fail if SAP SDK returns different structure
Missing test coverage for 20-result limit and invalid PublicKey format scenarios

strengths: Proper Zod validation on query parameters
Comprehensive error handling with specific error messages
Good logging with structured format matching project patterns
Correct integration with existing auth middleware
Well-structured tests covering main success/failure paths
TypeScript strict mode compliance - no 'any' types used
Follows existing routing patterns in app.ts
Good separation of concerns with clear route handlers
Handles edge cases like missing env vars and invalid PublicKey format

tasks.description: SAP x402 escrow bridge — detect SAP EscrowModule settlements on Solana and auto-create Invoica invoices
tasks._integrityDetails: File backend/src/services/settlement/sap-escrow-bridge.ts shrank from 429 to 155 lines (36%). Possible destructive rewrite.

review.summary: Multiple critical issues: incomplete file truncation, missing required integration, test failures, and specification violations

issues.description: File is truncated mid-function at line 155, ending with incomplete code 'SAP_SETTLE_'. This will cause compilation errors.
Required integration not implemented. Missing import of detectSapEscrowSettlement and missing integration code in settlement detection loop as specified.
Specification violation: Used discriminator parsing approach when task explicitly required SIMPLE APPROACH with 'do NOT use discriminator parsing'
Test will fail due to incomplete implementation and missing fetch import (uses global.fetch but imports from node-fetch in main file)
Over-engineered solution with unnecessary validation functions, crypto imports, and complex discriminator logic when simple checks were requested
Missing fetch import from 'node-fetch' as shown in task specification

strengths: Good TypeScript typing with proper interface definition

tasks.description: Draft @invoica_ai tweet thread announcing SAP integration — agent discovery, on-chain identity
tasks.context:
CMO SCOPE — Write the tweet thread as plain text in your response ONLY.
Do NOT create any files. Do NOT output file paths.

CONTEXT: Invoica now detects SAP escrow settlements on Solana.
When an agent pays via SAP x402 escrow, Invoica auto-detects the on-chain
settlement and creates the invoice automatically.

WRITE 3 TWEETS (plain text, no markdown headers, no code fences):

Tweet 1 — Headline (max 280 chars):
  Invoica now auto-detects SAP escrow settlements on Solana.
  When an agent pays via @OOBEProtocol x402 escrow, Invoica sees it on-chain
  and creates the invoice. SAP Program: SAPpUhsWLJG1FfkGRcXagEDMrMsWGjbky7AyhGpFETZ

Tweet 2 — How it works (max 280 chars):
  detectSapEscrowSettlement(txSig, rpcUrl)
  checks tx targets SAP program + USDC to Invoica wallet
  returns SapEscrowMatch | null. Never throws.

Tweet 3 — Why it matters (max 280 chars):
  SAP agents don't chase invoices. Pay via escrow, Invoica auto-invoices.
  Fully on-chain. Zero manual steps. One step toward the autonomous agent economy.

RULES: max 280 chars each, 1-2 emoji per tweet, tag @OOBEProtocol, technically precise.
Output ONLY the 3 tweets as plain numbered text. Nothing else.

review.summary: Task fundamentally misunderstood requirements and created unauthorized file with destructive rewrite

issues.description: Task spec explicitly stated 'Do NOT create any files' but agent created a markdown file anyway
File contains markdown code fence (```typescript) which is invalid syntax in source files
Destructive rewrite detected - file shrank from 17 to 3 lines (18% reduction), indicating loss of existing functionality
Agent PDA appears to be placeholder/fake: '5sN5YdCDXw7Rq3x2Q8hF4vLjK9mP6tN0wE2sR3yU7vX' - task required real PDA from SAP-001 output
Content structure doesn't match tweet format - includes markdown headers and separators instead of plain tweet text

strengths: Content includes required technical details (SAP Program ID, capabilities)
